source.title: Accident Investigation Report - High Island A 379 B Platform Confined Space Entry Incident

context.region: Gulf of Mexico - High Island Area Block A 379

actions_taken: IP evacuated by Medivac helicopter to Galveston hospital
IP placed in Intensive Care Unit for 3 days
event.summary: On September 4, 2023, at approximately 09:15 hours on High Island A 379 B platform, an injured person (IP) entered a Heater Treater vessel (NBK-1070) to clean it while wearing only an Air Purifying Respirator instead of a Supplied Air Respirator. The IP was overcome by fumes and found face down in fluids/sludge, not breathing with no pulse. Two supervisors entered without respirators to rescue the IP. CPR was administered for 20 minutes before the IP was revived. The incident resulted from multiple failures to follow the confined space entry plan including: no tri-gas monitoring, no hole watch, no rescue equipment setup, improper PPE, no entry permit, and inadequate training. The operator also failed to accurately report the severity of the incident to BSEE.
recommendations: Ensure all Subcontractors adhere to work plans
Ensure all Company and Subcontracted personnel are properly trained before prior to task/work is started
Ensure accurate incident reporting is made, to include follow up reports
key_phrases: face down in fluids and sludge
failed to follow confined space entry plan
Air Purifying Respirator instead of Supplied Air Respirator

hazards.name: Toxic hydrocarbon vapors and oxygen deficiency in heater treater vessel

threats.name: Unauthorized entry into confined space without proper controls
Inadequate atmospheric monitoring of confined space
Use of inadequate respiratory protection for confined space
Failure to establish hole watch during confined space entry

consequences.name: Worker exposure to toxic atmosphere causing loss of consciousness
Cardiac and respiratory arrest requiring CPR
Secondary exposure of rescue personnel during unplanned rescue
Serious injury requiring intensive care hospitalization

controls.name: Confined Space Entry Plan and Procedures
controls.barrier_role: Prevent unauthorized or unsafe entry into confined space
controls.lod_basis: Primary procedural control to prevent unsafe entry

supporting_text: The Investigation revealed the Lessee and their subcontractor Zealous Energy Services, failed to follow their confined space entry plan
Lessee and Subcontractor failure to follow Confined Space Entry Plan

controls.name: Confined Space Entry Permit System
controls.barrier_role: Authorize and document safe entry conditions

supporting_text: The IP made the vessel entry without the proper permit
Crew member making entry into vessel without permit
PIC not trained in Confined Space Entry yet is responsible issuing the permit

controls.name: Tri-Gas Atmospheric Monitoring (O2, H2S, LEL)
controls.barrier_role: Detect hazardous atmosphere before and during entry
controls.lod_basis: Primary detection system for atmospheric hazards

supporting_text: No Tri-gas survey was conducted when the Heater Treater vessel hatch was initially opened, and to continuous monitoring for Tri-gas readings
Failure to conduct Tri-Gas testing and continuous monitoring
No Tri-Gas (O2, H2S, LEL) initial and continuous monitoring conducted

controls.name: Supplied Air Respirator (SAR) for Confined Space Entry
controls.barrier_role: Protect worker from toxic atmosphere during entry
controls.lod_basis: Last line of defense for worker protection

supporting_text: the IP entered the vessel without a Supplied Air Respirator (SAR), and only wearing an Air Purifying Respirator (APR)
Improper Personal Protective Equipment
Personal Protective Equipment was not used properly or appropriate for task being conducted

controls.name: Hole Watch Personnel at Confined Space Opening
controls.barrier_role: Monitor entrant and initiate rescue if needed
controls.lod_basis: Secondary monitoring and emergency response initiation

supporting_text: the Lessee failed to designate an individual for Hole Watch at hatch opening
No Hole Watch identified and posted at hatch opening
No Hole watch individual identified as assigned at hatch opening

controls.name: Rescue Equipment Setup at Confined Space Location
controls.barrier_role: Enable rapid rescue of incapacitated entrant

supporting_text: have rescue equipment set up at the location of the vessel before and during the cleaning operation
Failed to have rescue equipment set up and at the location of the vessel before and during the cleaning operation
The two Supervisors made entry to rescue the IP without proper respiratory equipment, because of the equipment not being set up prior to the beginning of vessel cleaning operation

controls.name: Stop Work Authority
controls.barrier_role: Empower workers to halt unsafe operations
controls.lod_basis: Primary human intervention to prevent unsafe acts

supporting_text: Stop Work Authority not used
Stop work authority was not used

controls.name: Confined Space Entry Training and Competency Verification
controls.barrier_role: Ensure personnel understand and can execute safe entry procedures

supporting_text: PIC not trained in Confined Space Entry yet is responsible issuing the permit
No vetting of personnel for specific job qualifications
Ensure all Company and Subcontracted personnel are properly trained before prior to task/work is started
Vetting of personnel for specific job qualifications was not conducted appropriately

controls.name: CPR and Emergency Medical Response
controls.barrier_role: Restore vital functions and stabilize injured worker

supporting_text: the crew members found the IP not breathing, with no pulse, and began cardiopulmonary resuscitation (CPR) on the IP
After approximately 20 minutes of CPR, the IP began to breath on his own
CPR was administered for approximately 20 minutes to revive the IP

controls.name: Medical Evacuation (Medivac) Capability
controls.barrier_role: Transport critically injured worker to advanced medical care
controls.lod_basis: Emergency transport and escalation of care

supporting_text: The IP was flown to the hospital in Galveston, Texas by Medivac helicopter for treatment
awaiting medivac aircraft to take him in for medical evaluation

controls.name: Incident Reporting to Regulatory Authority (BSEE)
controls.barrier_role: Enable regulatory oversight and investigation of serious incidents

supporting_text: the Lessee's initial reporting at 12:15 hours on September 4, 2023, was misleading as to the seriousness of this incident
The Lessee never stated the seriousness of the IP's condition, and failed to mention the IP was in the Intensive Care Unit for a period of three (3) days
Lessee failed to verbally notify the Lake Jackson District immediately following incidents involving all injuries requiring evacuation
Lessee's initial and update reporting on the day of the incident, failed to report the severity of the incident to Lake Jackson District in accurate and timely manner

notes.rules: JSON output only. mentioned fields must be evidence-based. Use null for unknown values.
